entries.transcript: Big! and small, Tall and short. Big like a horse, small like a mouse, Tall like a tree, short like a house. Big and small, tall and short, Every size is just the right sort! Big! Small! Tall! Short! Sing it loud — we love them all!

Red like an apple, blue like the sky, Yellow like the sunshine way up high. Green like a leaf, and orange like a pear, Purple like a grape — colors everywhere! Black like the nighttime, white like a cloud, Sing the colors, sing them loud! Red and blue and yellow, green, Beautiful colors to be seen!

One little duckling, two little ducklings, Three little, four little, five little ducklings, Six little, seven little, eight little ducklings, Nine, ten ducklings in the pond! Count with me, one to ten, Count with me, then again! One, two, three, four, five, Six, seven, eight, nine, ten — alive!

Apple, banana, orange, grape, Strawberry, pear — what a shape! Toss them all in a great big bowl, Fruit salad friends — that's the goal! I like apples, I like pears, Sweet little fruits everywhere! Banana yellow, grape so blue, I like fruit and so do you! Strawberry red, orange round, Juicy flavors all around! Mix them up and share a plate, Fruit salad friends taste so great! I like apples, I like pears, Sweet little fruits everywhere! Apple, banana, orange, grape, Strawberry, pear — fruit-salad shape!

Hello, hello, hello, friend! Hi to you and hi again! Good morning, sunny day, Let's all learn and let's all play! Bye, bye, see you soon! Bye, bye, afternoon! Wave your hand and smile so bright, We'll be back, we're feeling right!

On Coco's farm the day begins, A cow steps out and moo, moo, moo. A sheep walks by and baa, baa, baa, A pig says oink — hello to you! On Coco's farm the friends all sing, Different voices, every one! A horse goes neigh, a duck goes quack, A chicken clucks beneath the sun. Cow and sheep and pig and horse, Duck and chicken — all together! On Coco's farm we sing along, Every voice and every feather. On Coco's farm we sing along, One big farm, one happy song!
Bea looks at the fruit bowl. So many colors!
She picks an apple. I like apples, says Bea.
Then Bea sees a green pear. A new fruit for her.
Bea takes a small bite. Soft and sweet. She smiles.
I like pears too, says Bea. New fruit, new friend.
What fruit was new for Bea?
Put the fruit story in order.
Bea Bunny's family goes to the park.
Mommy and Daddy carry the blanket.
Grandma brings the apples. Grandpa brings the bread.
Sister and brother share the fruit. Yum!
What did Mommy and Daddy carry?
Put the picnic story in order.
Bea Bunny opens the door. Welcome to her house!
Here is the kitchen. A big table and four chairs.
This is the bedroom. A soft bed and a sunny window.
And the living room. A lamp shines on the rug. Come in!
Where are the table and chairs?
How are you? I'm fine, thank you!
Pip the Parrot visits a sunny pet room.
Hello, cat! A small cat purrs on a soft mat.
Hi, dog! A happy dog wags its tail. Hello, rabbit! Hop, hop, hop.
A bird, a fish, and a hamster wave too. So many new friends!
Which pet did Pip meet first?
Ten little ducklings live by the pond.
One, two, three jump in first.
Seven, eight, nine glide so smooth.
Mommy duck smiles. What a happy pond!
Put the ducklings in the order they joined the swim.
Coco the Goat walks on the farm. She wants to hear a quiet sound.
The cow says moo. The sheep says baa. Not so quiet.
The pig says oink. The horse says neigh. Still not so quiet.
The duck says quack. The chicken says cluck. Loud and proud!
Coco peeks in the pond. A fish blows bubbles. Bubble, bubble. So quiet!
Every voice is welcome on the farm. Loud, soft, big, small — all friends.
Which animal made the quietest sound?
What sound does the horse make?
Put Coco's farm walk in order.
Good morning! Bea eats breakfast. Milk and bread.
At noon, Bea eats lunch. Soup and water.
In the evening, Bea eats dinner. Rice and juice.
Three meals, three smiles. Good night, Bea.
In the forest, the friends share what they can do.
I can fly, says the bird. Up, up, up!
I can swim, says the fish. Swish, swish, swish!
I can climb, says the bear. Up the big tree!
A fish can't walk. A cat can't fly. That's okay — we all do different things.
Everyone can do something. What can you do today?
Put the friends in the order they spoke.
Tap: a bird can't swim like a fish.
